name: Spinach Fettucine with Ginger Chicken-Daley
recipeIngredient: 1 medium Yellow squash, trimmed and cut into matchsticks
1 medium Red bell pepper, trimmed and cut into strips
1 large Leek, white part only, trimmed and cut into half rounds
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350 degrees.
2. In a blender, mix together all the marinade/dressing ingredients (starting with chopped shallot and ending with grated fresh ginger).
3. Place the chicken in a shallow baking dish. Pour half of the dressing over the poultry, reserving the remainder. Cover and refrigerate for 20 minutes.
4. Bring a large pot of water to boil over high heat. Add the fettucini and cook until desired doneness (8-12 minutes).
5. Remove the chicken from the refrigerator. Uncover the baking dish and transfer it to the oven. Bake for 15 minutes, or until the meat is no longer pink and juices run clear.
6. When the pasta is cooked, remove the pot from heat. Drain the fettucini and return it to the pot. Stir in the chicken stock and keep warm over the lowest heat setting.
7. Pour the cooking juices from the chicken into a sauté pan. Add the squash, bell pepper, leek, broccoli, and mushrooms. Sauté for about 3 minutes, just until vegetables begin to wilt.
8. Transfer the pasta to a serving bowl, along with the sautéed vegetables. Shred the cooked chicken on top. Add the reserved dressing and toss. Garnish with snipped chives.
